input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the `BindingContext`. The bindings have no source object to pull values from.

**Fix — set the BindingContext**, either in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:YourViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Or in the code-behind constructor:

```csharp
public MyPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new YourViewModel();
}
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or uses `[ObservableProperty]` from the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI data binding issue where labels appear blank despite the ViewModel having values. This is a common issue - they're missing the `BindingContext` assignment.

Let me give a concise answer.
